context: Tests whether the agent produces a talk outline that follows the presentation-creator skill's structural conventions: the Presentation Spec block, opening sequence format, typed numbered placeholders (including meme briefs), callback flags, cut line markers, and closing sequence — all from a provided vault and conference brief.

checklist.name: Presentation Spec block
checklist.description: Output includes a PRESENTATION SPEC block (or equivalent structured summary) containing at least 5 of the following 7 fields: Title, Thesis, Audience, Venue, Mode, Duration target, Shownotes slug

checklist.name: Section time + slide range headers
checklist.description: Each major section of the outline includes both a time allocation (e.g., [10 min]) and a slide range (e.g., slides 5-14) in the section header or opening line

checklist.name: Opening sequence structure
checklist.description: The opening sequence contains at minimum three of the following slide types: title slide, bio slide, shownotes URL slide (with QR code mentioned), and opening hook slide

checklist.name: Multiple typed placeholder categories
checklist.description: The outline uses at least two distinct typed placeholder categories with independent numbering — e.g., [AUTHOR 01] and [MEME 01] are both present, with each type starting its own numbering sequence from 01

checklist.name: Meme brief format
checklist.description: At least one [MEME XX] placeholder is expanded into a structured brief containing all four sub-fields: Template, Search query, Overlay text, and Rhetorical function

checklist.name: CUT LINE marker
checklist.description: The outline contains at least one [CUT LINE] marker (or equivalent, e.g., '## [CUT LINE:...]') indicating where content can be dropped for a shorter slot

checklist.name: Callback flags
checklist.description: At least one callback opportunity is explicitly flagged using [CALLBACK: ...], [PROGRESSIVE LIST: ...], or [RUNNING GAG: ...] notation — not just mentioned in prose

checklist.name: Speaker notes in outline
checklist.description: At least half of the slide entries include a 'Speaker:' line with actual sample speaker text of 10+ words (not just '[speaker notes]', '[no notes]', or empty) — showing what the speaker would actually say

checklist.name: Closing sequence completeness
checklist.description: The outline's closing section contains distinct slides (or slide descriptions) for all three components: a summary/recap, a call-to-action (CTA), and a social/contact slide with shownotes URL

checklist.name: Slide count within budget
checklist.description: The total number of slides in the outline does not exceed 1.5 slides per minute of the stated duration (e.g., for a 45-minute talk: max 68 slides)
